message:
feat(wezterm): Cx-first get_pane + send_text family (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Extends the tick 19 list_panes_with_cx pattern to get_pane and the
complete send_text family on WeztermHandle. Eight additional public
Cx-first entry points land in this commit; each delegates to pool
`_with_cx` variants on the mux-pool fast path and falls back to the
legacy ambient-Cx CLI path for recovery.

Scope:

1. crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs (+148 lines)

   New Cx-first public entry points (all asupersync-runtime gated):

     * `get_pane_with_cx(cx, pane_id)` — delegates to
       `list_panes_with_cx(cx)` so the pane lookup respects caller Cx.

     * `send_text_with_cx(cx, pane_id, text)` — paste mode + newline
       default (matches legacy `send_text`).

     * `send_text_no_paste_with_cx(cx, pane_id, text)` — no-paste mode
       with newline.

     * `send_text_with_options_with_cx(cx, pane_id, text, no_paste, no_newline)`
       — full control over paste/newline flags.

     * `send_control_with_cx(cx, pane_id, control_char)` — always
       no-paste + no-newline (required for control chars).

     * `send_ctrl_c_with_cx(cx, pane_id)` — convenience wrapper.

     * `send_ctrl_d_with_cx(cx, pane_id)` — convenience wrapper.

   New internal helper with two cfg-gated variants:

     * `send_text_impl_with_cx` (vendored+unix+asupersync) — uses
       `pool.send_paste_with_cx(cx)` / `pool.write_to_pane_with_cx(cx)`
       on the mux-pool fast path (both already Cx-first from earlier
       ticks). CLI fallback delegates to legacy `send_text_impl`.

     * `send_text_impl_with_cx` (asupersync w/o vendored+unix) — stub
       that calls legacy `send_text_impl` so the Cx-first API is
       available on all asupersync builds.

   Legacy methods preserved; strictly additive.

Impact: WeztermHandle now has 8 Cx-first entry points (list_panes +
get_pane + 6 send_text family). All production hot-path calls from
workflows, tailers, and the robot API can thread caller `&Cx`
directly to the mux-pool fast path without needing ambient-Cx
lookup. The retry/circuit-breaker stack remains ambient-Cx; full
migration there would affect every WeztermHandle async method and
is out of scope.

Verification (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):

  * cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib wezterm::: 160/160 pass
    (no new tests — covered by pre-existing pool `_with_cx` tests
    via delegation)
  * cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --lib -D warnings: clean
  * rustfmt --check: clean

Leaves ft-xbnl0.2.3 still in_progress. WeztermHandle Cx-first
coverage expanding: 8 of ~22 public async methods now have _with_cx
variants. Remaining (spawn, activate_pane, get_pane_direction,
split_pane, get_text, pane_tiered_scrollback_summary, etc.) can
follow the same mechanical pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
